  • [ - ] alx30 - member since 10-Apr-19

    Stats suggest Germany was quite unlucky (xg 3.1-1,4; 26 vs 12 shots; 5 vs 3 big chances). But when you're always this unlucky maybe the problem is your mentality. It seems every time things go right they become complacent and every time the pressure is on, this team can't score goals anymore, hides and keeps making mistakes at the back. Not everyone, but too many of them. You can't win anything like that.

    @BenMcAleer1 I think something must have changed in measuring dribbles by Opta lately. If you look at the stats of all other games you hardly see any numbers above 5 for any team.   • [ - ] Big_Boss - member since 19-Nov-16

    @Icarax It's not a matter what they deserve, the point I was making is that nobody can really tell wether you're joking or not. Hence why I couldn't tell that you were just doing it for fun. 'Petty BS'? That's new. I regard BS as BS without any degrees and that's where we differ. You resorted to argumentum ad hominem - which I found amusing and indeed, that compelled me to reply (without feeling offended since I know what the real deal is). The difference between your attempt to comprehend and me sharing thougts on a game is the dispassion showcased in my case. An unbiased pov where there is no room for prejudice, understanding & accepting the rules of the game and leaving room for corrections is my way of delivering commentary on the matches. Yours seem to stem from emotion which is normal, but makes for skewed projections. Again, my original points are this: when doing predictions or even when you tackle life itself, don't believe in absolutes all the time.

  • [ - ] Big_Boss - member since 19-Nov-16

    @Icarax You're right, it wasn't petty bs. You dont need to prove an axiom in order to give sound analysis. When there is ample room for interpretation then of course it's up for debate. Indeed we are humans with emotions, but you can adhere to solid reasoning (=sufficiently unbiased) in your views when you scrutinize events in the games. It's also why I like to defuse absurd talk on social media by giving facts and context (= just as important) to combat misconceptions. The same goes in work environments where the mind takes precedent over compassion. With football it helps to be as neutral as possible. Granted, not many people are able to seperate their preferences from objectivity, it's why the world is in the (poor) state it is in now. Thanks for being a good guy in this.
